assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address they can use to register.
No Mapbox account, access token, product family, or application platform has been selected yet.
The aspirational Maps first-success outcome is an interactive map that responds to drag and zoom.

developer_goal: Create a Mapbox account and reach the earliest official interactive first-map success for a selected Maps platform route.

first_success.outcome: An interactive Mapbox map is rendered and responds to drag and zoom.
first_success.observable_signal: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input in the selected web, iOS, or Android runtime.

route_selection.surface: Mapbox Getting Started and Map Developer Journey
route_selection.rule: Begin at account creation, then select one product family and, for Maps, one application platform and dependency variant before counting. Do not invent a winner among consequential platform ties.

candidates.name: Maps web with Mapbox GL JS CDN
candidates.reason: Shortest documented standalone web candidate from default public token through an interactive Standard-style map, but Choosing it requires fixing the product and platform fork and still lacks a verified signup-field transcript.

candidates.name: Maps web with npm or scaffolded project
candidates.reason: Peer GL JS install variants change dependency and project-setup actions for the same interactive-map outcome.

candidates.name: Maps SDK for iOS or Android
candidates.reason: Official Add a map stage presents these as peer Get Started guides with different toolchains, credentials placement, and runtimes.

candidates.name: Search, Navigation, Location AI, or data products
candidates.reason: Getting Started lists these as peer product families with different first-success signals from a rendered map.

required_path.action: Create a free Mapbox account from the official signup surface.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and credentials
required_fields.notes: Public docs state signup creates an account and default token; the signup page marketing copy does not enumerate required form fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated Mapbox account and can open the Developer Console.

required_path.action: Locate and copy the automatically created default public access token from the Developer Console.
required_path.observable_result: A valid pk. public token is available for the selected application guide.

required_path.action: Choose the Maps product family and one application platform plus install variant from the official Get Started guides.
required_path.observable_result: One official first-map guide is selected.

required_path.action: Follow the selected guide through dependency setup, token configuration, map container or view setup, and map instantiation.

required_fields.label: Public access token in application code or platform config
Platform-specific project and dependency setup
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on the unresolved web CDN, npm, iOS, or Android selection.

required_path.observable_result: The application is ready to run with Mapbox map rendering configured.

required_path.action: Run or open the application and drag and zoom the rendered map.
required_path.observable_result: The map is visible and responds to drag and zoom input.

prerequisites.description: A Mapbox account whose default public access token can authenticate Maps SDK or GL JS requests.
The selected platform toolchain: a web page and browser, Xcode and an iOS project, or Android Studio and an Android project.
Network access for CDN or package resources, styles, and tiles at first render.

external_gates.description: Account signup and any identity-provider or email verification steps required by the live Mapbox registration flow.

unavoidable_waits.description: Map style, tile, and SDK or CDN resources must load before the interactive map can be verified.

platform_outcomes.description: Mapbox automatically creates a default public access token when the account is created.
Mapbox GL JS or the selected Maps SDK renders the Mapbox Standard map and accepts drag and zoom interaction.

excluded.item: Adding custom data after the first interactive map
excluded.reason: Map Developer Journey places Add data after the first-map milestone.

excluded.item: Creating additional public or secret tokens
excluded.reason: Default public token is documented as sufficient to start.

excluded.item: Mapbox Studio style authoring and production token restriction

sources.title: Getting Started with Mapbox GL JS
Get started with Mapbox GL JS using a CDN
Get Started with Maps SDK for iOS
Get Started with Maps SDK for Android
evidence_supported: Android install path peer to web and iOS

sources.title: Mapbox signup
evidence_supported: No enumerated form fields in fetched HTML

uncertainties.question: Which Mapbox product family and, for Maps, which application platform and dependency variant define the selected first-success route?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes toolchain, credential placement, required code, waits, and the runtime where drag-and-zoom is observed.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy commit to one Maps platform guide, then a complete step transcript for that guide from account creation through interactive map.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, verification steps, or billing-card gates appear on a fresh Mapbox account before the default public token is usable?
uncertainties.impact: Undocumented signup or payment gates change required fields, external gates, and whether the account step can be verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated signup-screen transcript or official field list; live credential submission is out of scope for this audit.
